[[File:PCL.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The ''Black Lotus'']]
The '''''Black Lotus''''' was a modified [[A-class bulk freighter|PCL 27]] container ship used by the [[Camarina Connection]].

==Characteristics==
The ''Black Lotus'' carried an operational crew of sixty, but scientists, technicians and enforcers increased her overall complement to 200. She was armed with twenty [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries—six each to port and starboard, four each fore and aft—and carried a heterogeneous swarm of fighters, including four [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptors]], three standard [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]], two [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighters]], one [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighter]] and five [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]].

==History==
Originally designed as a colony vessel, the [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] converted the ''Black Lotus'' to serve as a mobile factory for the combat drug [[pentabenzedralyne]]. To meet the energy needs of the production process, the vessel's engines were enhanced with two retractable arrays carrying photonic collectors, while two retractable platforms were added to the ventral hull, each outfitted with a row of hoppers to transfer the drug to smugglers' freighters.

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Black Lotus'' appears only in the French roleplaying-game magazine [[Casus Belli 89|''Casus Belli'' 89]], but the PCL 27 later appeared in [[Heir to the Empire 6|issue 6]] of the [[Star Wars: Heir to the Empire (comic series)|''Heir to the Empire'']] comic-book adaptation.
